agg.basalt: Basalt – hard rock, always crushed, very dense
agg.bauwerksplitt: Structural recycled aggregate – from demolition structures
agg.betonsplitt: Concrete recycled aggregate – from demolition concrete
agg.blahton: Expanded clay – lightweight aggregate for LWC
agg.kalkstein: Dense limestone – usually as crushed limestone
agg.kiessand: Gravel sand (Quartz) – standard from gravel pit, rounded ✓

cem.CEM_III_B_42.5_N: CEM III/B 42.5 N – blast-furnace cement, very low heat

cons.F1: F1 – stiff, simple foundations without reinforcement
cons.F3: F3 – soft, standard for most applications ✓
cons.F4: F4 – very soft, flow agent required
cons.F6: F6 – very flowable, flow agent required
cons.hint.C0: Very dry, hardly processable by hand. Only for vibration tables or precast.
cons.hint.F1: Dry concrete, holds shape without formwork. For simple pathway slabs or paving bedding.
cons.hint.F2: Compactable with poker bar. Typical for foundations and floor slabs.
cons.hint.F3: Flows easily into formwork, easy to work with. DIY standard.
cons.hint.F4: Very flowable – flow agent (FM) mandatory. For heavily reinforced elements.
cons.hint.F5: Self-compacting – flow agent (FM) mandatory. For architectural concrete.
cons.hint.F6: Very fluid – flow agent (FM) mandatory. Special applications.
cons.hint.fm.required: Flow agent (FM) is mandatory – please select FM under "Superplasticizer".
effect.flyash: Fly ash {pct}% – reduces cement demand and improves durability.
effect.lp: Air entrainer {airPct}% reduces water demand and strength. (approx. {loss} N/mm²)
effect.silica: Silica fume {pct}% – significantly increases strength and tightness.

index.hint.waterproofing2: Used to reduce water permeability and improve frost resistance.

uhpc.note.body: High-performance concrete (UHPC) lies outside the industrial standard for normal concrete. The recipes offered here are from the publications linked in the source attribution and are scaled linearly to your volume. CreteLab does not estimate strength for UHPC — the actual values depend on factors such as mixing time, compaction and possibly fiber reinforcement, and are stated in the source attribution where known.
uhpc.preset.hint: Published DIY and research recipes with source attribution.
uhpc.preset.label: Base recipe:
uhpc.volume.hint: Reference points: small bowl ≈ 0.005 m³, planter ≈ 0.02 m³, tabletop 80 × 40 × 4 cm ≈ 0.013 m³.
uhpc.source.author: Author
uhpc.source.title: Title

warn.fines.too.high: Fines content {fines} kg/m³ too high – maximum {maxAllowed} kg/m³ for exposure class {klasse}.
warn.flyash.silica: Fly ash content too high for combined use with silica fume with this cement – please reduce proportions.
warn.high.air: Air content {pct}% is unusually high – this may significantly reduce strength.
warn.paste.too.low: Paste content {paste} kg/m³ is too low for exposure class {klasse}.
warn.strength.too.low: Concrete class {klasse} is too weak for selected exposure classes – at least {minFck} N/mm² cube strength required.
warn.wz.exceeded: Water-cement ratio {wzc} exceeds the allowable limit {maxWz} of selected exposure classes.
index.hint.air.entailing: Recommended for frost-exposed components (e.g., outdoor areas). Higher air content reduces water demand but also reduces compressive strength.
fine.tune.beton.verf.desc: Reduces water demand by approx. 7%, the concrete flows more evenly into the formwork. Available at hardware stores. Excludes flow agent.
index.hint.cement.type: Available at hardware stores: CEM I 42.5 N (Portland cement, standard recommendation). CEM II is suitable for less demanding applications, CEM III for sulfate-resistant or lower-heat mixes.

uhpc.preset.diy-pce-30l-batch.label: DIY High-Performance Concrete (with Quartz Flour & Microsilica)
uhpc.preset.diy-pce-30l-batch.step.1: Pre-mix dry ingredients ({cementKg} Cement + {sandKg} Sand + {quartzPowderKg} Quartz Flour + {microsilicaKg} Microsilica) — mix well.
uhpc.preset.diy-pce-30l-batch.step.2: Dissolve PCE superplasticizer in the mixing water ({superplasticizerL} PCE in {waterL} Water).
uhpc.preset.diy-pce-30l-batch.step.3: Slowly add the water-PCE mixture to the dry mix and mix vigorously for at least 5 minutes — flow properties develop slowly.
uhpc.preset.diy-pce-30l-batch.step.4: Pour into oiled form and vibrate or tap gently until no more air bubbles rise.
uhpc.preset.diy-pce-30l-batch.step.5: Cover and keep moist for at least 24 h, carefully demold, let cure for several days.
uhpc.preset.diy-mortar-20kg-batch.label: DIY High-Strength Mortar (without Quartz Flour)
uhpc.preset.diy-mortar-20kg-batch.step.1: Mix cement and dry sand with about one third of the mixing water ({cementKg} Cement + {sandKg} Sand + approx. 0.8 l Water). Sand must be dry — moist sand shifts the w/c ratio.
uhpc.preset.diy-mortar-20kg-batch.step.2: Dissolve PCE superplasticizer in the remaining water ({superplasticizerL} PCE in the remaining approx. 1.6 l Water).
uhpc.preset.diy-mortar-20kg-batch.step.3: Add the water-PCE mixture in stages and mix vigorously for at least 5 minutes — ideally in a forced-action mixer. The superplasticizer only reaches full effect after even distribution.
uhpc.preset.diy-mortar-20kg-batch.step.4: Pour into oiled form and vibrate or tap gently until no more air bubbles rise.
uhpc.preset.diy-mortar-20kg-batch.step.5: Cover and keep moist for at least 24 h, carefully demold, let cure for several days.
uhpc.preset.kassel-m1q-cem42-5r.label: Research Fine-Grain Concrete (full PCE dosage)
uhpc.preset.kassel-m1q-cem42-5r.step.1: Thoroughly homogenize the dry mix: {cementKg} Cement + {sandKg} Sand (0.125–0.5 mm) + {microsilicaKg} Microsilica + {quartzPowderKg} Quartz Flour. For UHPC, mixing the fine powders evenly is critical — mix dry for at least 2 minutes.
uhpc.preset.kassel-m1q-cem42-5r.step.2: Dissolve PCE superplasticizer in the mixing water ({superplasticizerL} PCE in {waterL} Water).
uhpc.preset.kassel-m1q-cem42-5r.step.3: Add the water-PCE mixture in stages to the dry mix and mix vigorously for 5–10 minutes total — ideally in a forced-action mixer. Warning: Fresh concrete temperature can rise to 40 °C in large batches — the mix will stiffen faster.
uhpc.preset.kassel-m1q-cem42-5r.step.4: Pour into oiled form and compact with a standard immersion vibrator until the concrete levelles itself.
uhpc.preset.kassel-m1q-cem42-5r.step.5: Demold after 24–48 hours (depending on superplasticizer retardation), then keep covered moist for at least one week (e.g. under wet cloth + plastic wrap). Compressive strength after 28 d: approx. 100 N/mm².
uhpc.preset.kassel-m1q-cem42-5r.step.6: Optional for maximum strength: Store completely underwater at 20 °C for 28 days → ~123 N/mm² (+23%). With this very dense mix, the water bath effect is particularly strong against self-drying of the matrix.
uhpc.preset.kassel-m1q-cem42-5r-soft.lab: Research Fine-Grain Concrete (moderate PCE dosage, for indoor use)
uhpc.preset.kassel-m1q-cem42-5r-soft.ste: Thoroughly homogenize the dry mix: {cementKg} Cement + {sandKg} Sand (0.125–0.5 mm) + {microsilicaKg} Microsilica + {quartzPowderKg} Quartz Flour. Mix dry for at least 2 minutes — fine powder distribution determines final strength.
Dissolve PCE superplasticizer in the mixing water ({superplasticizerL} PCE in {waterL} Water). This variant works with a DIY-typical PCE dosage.
Add the water-PCE mixture in stages to the dry mix and mix for 5–10 minutes. Flow properties develop slowly — initially "grainy", then smooth after a few minutes.
Pour into oiled form and compact with a standard immersion vibrator until the surface shines.
Demold after 24–48 h, then keep covered moist for at least one week. Compressive strength after 28 d: approx. 95 N/mm².

Optional for maximum strength: Store completely underwater at 20 °C for 28 days → ~103 N/mm² (+8%). With this slightly less dense variant (w/c = 0.40), the water bath effect is smaller but noticeable.
pl.pce.err: Above the datasheet window (0.3–4.0 %) — check your PCE product, may need a different type.
fine.tune.meta.tw.desc: Add additives to your concrete recipe: fly ash, concrete plasticizer, silica fume or air-entraining agent.
fine.tune.card.cement.desc: 10% more cement in the same mix – the easiest way to noticeably more compressive strength. No special product, available at hardware stores.
fine.tune.card.flyash.desc: Additive from power plant ash. Improves long-term strength, makes concrete denser and slightly more fluid. Available at building material suppliers.
fine.tune.card.fm.desc: Stronger plasticizer: reduces water demand by approx. 20%. For very flowable concrete (F4–F6). Check dosage per product. Excludes concrete plasticizer.
fine.tune.card.lp.desc: Creates fine air pores that prevent frost damage. Important for terraces, driveways and outdoor stairs. Reduces compressive strength by approx. 14 N/mm².
fine.tune.card.silica.desc: Microsilica makes concrete noticeably stronger and denser. For heavily loaded floors, columns and precast elements. Only available at building material suppliers or online (special product).
fine.tune.combine.warn: ⚠️ Silica fume is not recommended for frost-exposed concrete (with air-entraining agent). Please use only one of the two.
